# [Clang] Crash in `clang::NamedDecl::printQualifiedName`  when instantiating nested lambdas in default template arguments

**Author:** Xniao
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173139

## Body

**_Generated by fuzzer._**
Repro: https://godbolt.org/z/caaWeenn7
Clang trunk (and versions since 13.0) crashes with a segmentation fault (exit code 139) when compiling following code using `-std=c++20`. 

While the syntax structure is almost identical to issue #152689 (nested lambdas within default template arguments), the stack traces reveal different failure points:

#152689: Crashes during name mangling (Backend/Mangle) when processing a local class.

This case: Crashes during semantic analysis (Frontend/Sema) when computing names for PredefinedExpr (e.g., assert).
## Program
```cpp
#include <cassert>
template <class F = decltype([] <auto G = [] {
    assert(true);
}> () {})>
void f(F op = {}) { op(); }

int main() { f(); }
```
